added_assumptions.label: ass:clipped-score-evaluation
added_assumptions.statement: \begin{assumption}[Clipped score evaluation]\label{ass:clipped-score-evaluation} For each fold, the score in $\hat\theta_n^{\mathrm{locDR}}$ is evaluated with $\hat g^c_{a,-k}=\Pi_{[-M,M]}\hat g_{a,-k}$ for $a\in\{0,1\}$ and $\hat e^c_{-k}=\Pi_{[\epsilon/2,1-\epsilon/2]}\hat e_{-k}$, where $\Pi_{[a,b]}(u)=\min\{b,\max\{a,u\}\}$. The local nuisance-rate bounds in Assumption~\ref{ass:nuisance} are understood for these clipped score inputs. \end{assumption}
added_assumptions.source: stage0_k:upper proposed caveat (tentative_classification=caveat); granted by Opus supervisor 2026-06-04. Standard propensity/outcome trimming implied by overlap (ass:causal: e in [eps,1-eps]) + boundedness (ass:bounded: |Y|,|g|<=M); non-load-bearing variance-control device, not an assumed construction.

proposed_from.topic: Structure-agnostic minimax rate for pointwise conditional-average-treatment-effect estimation: localize the scalar structure-agnostic linear-functional lower-bound theory to CATE at a point x0 under black-box nuisance rates (r_g, r_m), proving a matching cross-fit DR-Learner achiever, i.e. a sqrt(r_g r_m)-type pointwise rate with both achievability and a Le Cam two-point converse.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"The closest in-catalogue signal is eid_dml_orthogonality_frontier_v1, whose reviewer asked for primitive empirical-process upper bounds and minimax lower-bound arguments rather than assumed frontier channels. This proposal narrows the target to fixed-x0 Stat CATE estimation and asks for a matched pointwise rate rho_n with explicit localization, sampling, and nuisance-product channels.","published_axis":"KennedyBalakrishnanRobinsWasserman2024MinimaxCATE provides the closest pointwise CATE minimax comparator under Holder smoothness; JinSyrgkanis2024StructureAgnosticDR and JinSyrgkanis2025GeneralLinearFunctionalLowerBounds provide the closest structure-agnostic DR and lower-bound technology. The proposed gap is the localized nonregular CATE point target with effective local sample size p_n, localization bias b_n, and a black-box sqrt(r_g r_m) nuisance-product term. The selective-DR background citation has been corrected to CuiTchetgenTchetgen2024SelectiveDR."}
seed_list: Construct a cross-fit local DR-Learner for CATE(x0) and prove the matched structure-agnostic pointwise rate b_n+(n p_n)^(-1/2)+sqrt(r_g r_m) with a three-channel Le Cam converse.
Characterize the extra rate channel created by learning the local Riesz representer for CATE(x0), and prove an iff condition under which the rate remains b_n+(n p_n)^(-1/2)+sqrt(r_g r_m).
Build a growing local CATE report class H_n whose orthogonal-score process violates Gaussian approximation at the entropy-Riesz boundary, giving a non-corner lower-bound witness.
Derive a computable bandwidth frontier h_star where pointwise local sampling error and nuisance-product error exchange dominance in the structure-agnostic CATE rate.
Construct a structure-agnostic uniform CATE band over x with width indexed by local effective sample size, nuisance rates, and index complexity, plus a canonical lower bound.
Prove an adaptive oracle inequality for selected nuisance learners and bandwidths in a pointwise local DR-Learner, with a lower bound for any unavoidable selection cost.
Show that automatic Riesz regression for pointwise CATE is equivalent to fixed-localizer DR learning up to a learned-representer error term, and characterize when the term is negligible.
State and solve a localized calibration criterion for pointwise DR-Learners that preserves the sqrt(r_g r_m) product term while selecting among nuisance libraries.
Give a local quantile-to-mean bridge showing when LDML target-dependent nuisance localization for quantiles transports to pointwise CATE without changing the product-rate channel.
Construct a black-box localizer theorem comparing forest weights and kernel weights for pointwise CATE, with a threshold where both attain the same structure-agnostic rate.

seed_details.one_line: Construct a cross-fit local DR-Learner for CATE(x0) and prove the matched structure-agnostic pointwise rate b_n+(n p_n)^(-1/2)+sqrt(r_g r_m) with a three-channel Le Cam converse.

anchor_paper.author: Jin and Syrgkanis
anchor_paper.claim: ATE/ATT structure-agnostic DR optimality leaves pointwise CATE outside the target class.
anchor_paper.location: paper introduction and abstract; target list excludes point CATE

seed_details.anchor_followup: The paper poses black-box nuisance optimality for global effects; the seed localizes the unresolved pointwise CATE follow-up.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: CATE point version of ATE result; nontrivial because point localization changes variance and lower-bound geometry.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from JinSyrgkanis2025GeneralLinearFunctionalLowerBounds because CATE(x0) is just a local linear functional.
seed_details.rebuttal: The general lower-bound comparator does not instantiate the CATE local effective sample size, localization bias, and mixed nuisance pair together.

seed_details.one_line: Characterize the extra rate channel created by learning the local Riesz representer for CATE(x0), and prove an iff condition under which the rate remains b_n+(n p_n)^(-1/2)+sqrt(r_g r_m).

anchor_paper.author: Chernozhukov, Newey, and Singh
anchor_paper.claim: Local nonregular functionals need regularized Riesz representers; sharp CATE product-rate preservation with learned representers is not characterized.
anchor_paper.location: paper abstract and local functional examples

seed_details.anchor_followup: The paper treats local Riesz inference; the seed asks when learned local representers preserve the structure-agnostic CATE frontier.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extension/generalization warning; nontrivial content is an iff rate-preservation condition, not domain reuse.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from the cited method exemplar because it already gives orthogonal local inference under nuisance-rate conditions.
seed_details.rebuttal: The exemplar gives upper inference conditions, not a necessary-and-sufficient CATE-local learned-representer frontier.

seed_details.one_line: Build a growing local CATE report class H_n whose orthogonal-score process violates Gaussian approximation at the entropy-Riesz boundary, giving a non-corner lower-bound witness.

anchor_paper.author: prior proposal
anchor_paper.claim: Prior reviewer asked for a concrete non-corner growing report-packing witness.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: Add a concrete non-corner report-packing witness for Conjecture 1 with a specified growing class H_n

seed_details.anchor_followup: The prior gap asks for the witness missing from the local CATE orthogonality frontier.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/witness warning; nontrivial because the witness is a growing non-corner report class, not a fixed finite example.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Gaussian approximation theory because entropy already controls the score process.
seed_details.rebuttal: The prior failure was exactly the missing local CATE packing witness at the entropy-Riesz scale.

seed_details.one_line: Derive a computable bandwidth frontier h_star where pointwise local sampling error and nuisance-product error exchange dominance in the structure-agnostic CATE rate.

anchor_paper.author: Kennedy et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Holder-smooth CATE minimax theory gives local rates but not a black-box rate frontier in h_n, p_n, r_g, r_m.

seed_details.anchor_followup: The structured rate suggests a bandwidth frontier, but the structure-agnostic rate boundary is not isolated.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime warning; nontrivial content is a computable threshold h_star, not routine bandwidth algebra.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from balancing bias and variance in Kennedy et al. because h is just a smoothing parameter.
seed_details.rebuttal: The product nuisance term creates a third channel absent from the ordinary smooth oracle balance.

seed_details.one_line: Construct a structure-agnostic uniform CATE band over x with width indexed by local effective sample size, nuisance rates, and index complexity, plus a canonical lower bound.

anchor_paper.author: Semenova and Chernozhukov
anchor_paper.claim: DML gives simultaneous inference for CATE projections, not nonprojected structure-agnostic pointwise bands.
anchor_paper.location: paper abstract and simultaneous inference setup

seed_details.anchor_followup: The projection band result invites a nonprojected local band theory under black-box nuisance rates.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Uniformization warning; nontrivial only if the band has an index-complexity lower bound, not just a pointwise union bound.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from SemenovaChernozhukov2021DMLCATE by replacing basis functions with local indicators.
seed_details.rebuttal: The projection target avoids nonregular point evaluation and does not supply local effective-sample lower bounds.

seed_details.one_line: Prove an adaptive oracle inequality for selected nuisance learners and bandwidths in a pointwise local DR-Learner, with a lower bound for any unavoidable selection cost.

anchor_paper.author: Benkeser, Cai, and van der Laan
anchor_paper.claim: Selective nuisance learning is developed for finite-dimensional DR functionals, not local pointwise CATE rates.
anchor_paper.location: paper abstract and pseudo-risk selection setup

seed_details.anchor_followup: The practical learner-selection problem remains open when bandwidth and point localization enter the DR remainder.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Selection-extension warning; nontrivial only if paired with an oracle inequality and lower-bound cost.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from selective DR because the pseudo-risk can be localized.
seed_details.rebuttal: Localization changes both the target and bias-variance-nuisance balance, so the finite-dimensional pseudo-risk argument is not enough.

seed_details.one_line: Show that automatic Riesz regression for pointwise CATE is equivalent to fixed-localizer DR learning up to a learned-representer error term, and characterize when the term is negligible.

anchor_paper.author: Chernozhukov et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Riesz regression automates representer learning, leaving pointwise CATE localizer equivalence and error transfer open.
anchor_paper.location: paper abstract and Riesz regression contribution

seed_details.anchor_followup: The paper asks the user to choose the target functional; the seed makes pointwise CATE the target and studies the rate transfer.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Characterize-an-estimator warning; nontrivial only if the learned-representer error is an explicit term with a vanishing condition.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Riesz regression because the pointwise CATE functional has a Riesz representer.
seed_details.rebuttal: For point evaluation the representer is nonregular and localization-dependent, so the fixed-localizer equivalence requires a new error transfer.

seed_details.one_line: State and solve a localized calibration criterion for pointwise DR-Learners that preserves the sqrt(r_g r_m) product term while selecting among nuisance libraries.

anchor_paper.author: Benkeser, Cai, and van der Laan
anchor_paper.claim: Pseudo-risk nuisance selection targets finite-dimensional DR functionals and not localized CATE calibration.
anchor_paper.location: paper abstract and pseudo-risk selection setup

seed_details.anchor_followup: The follow-up is to localize the pseudo-risk while preserving the product-rate remainder.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Sensitivity/calibration warning; nontrivial content is a critical localized pseudo-risk criterion, not a new validation heuristic.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from selective DR after multiplying the influence function by K_h.
seed_details.rebuttal: The localizer changes the variance and bias terms, and selection must preserve the pointwise product channel.

seed_details.one_line: Give a local quantile-to-mean bridge showing when LDML target-dependent nuisance localization for quantiles transports to pointwise CATE without changing the product-rate channel.

anchor_paper.author: Kallus, Mao, and Uehara
anchor_paper.claim: Localized DML handles target-dependent nuisances for local quantile effects, not pointwise CATE minimax rates.
anchor_paper.location: paper abstract and localized nuisance setup

seed_details.anchor_followup: The localized nuisance idea can be transported only if the CATE product remainder survives the change of target.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Bridge warning; nontrivial only if both directions of the rate-transfer condition are proved.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from LDML because both problems use target-dependent nuisance localization.
seed_details.rebuttal: Quantile and mean targets have different scores and remainder terms; the product-rate preservation condition is not automatic.

seed_details.one_line: Construct a black-box localizer theorem comparing forest weights and kernel weights for pointwise CATE, with a threshold where both attain the same structure-agnostic rate.

anchor_paper.author: Athey, Tibshirani, and Wager
anchor_paper.claim: Generalized random forests give adaptive local weights with asymptotic theory under forest-specific conditions.
anchor_paper.location: paper abstract and local moment target setup

seed_details.anchor_followup: Forest inference motivates asking whether black-box localizers have the same rate frontier as kernels.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Estimator equivalence warning; nontrivial only if a computable threshold separates equality and divergence.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from causal forest asymptotic normality because forests are adaptive kernels.
seed_details.rebuttal: Forest honesty conditions are estimator-specific and do not characterize the black-box nuisance-product frontier.

proposed_from.literature_map:
Jin and Syrgkanis (2024, arXiv:2402.14264, https://arxiv.org/abs/2402.14264) prove structure-agnostic optimality for DR estimators of ATE, ATT, and weighted ATE, not pointwise CATE.
Jin and Syrgkanis (2025, arXiv:2512.17341, https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.17341) is a close newer comparator for general structure-agnostic functional lower bounds.
Kennedy, Balakrishnan, Robins, and Wasserman (2024, Annals of Statistics, DOI 10.1214/24-AOS2369) prove Holder-smooth pointwise CATE minimax rates.
Kennedy (2023, EJS/arXiv:2004.14497, https://arxiv.org/abs/2004.14497) gives the closest DR-CATE upper-bound and pseudo-outcome skeleton.
Chernozhukov et al. (2018, Econometrics Journal) supply the DML cross-fit orthogonal expansion and product-remainder template.
Chernozhukov, Newey, and Singh (2022, Econometrics Journal) treat regularized Riesz representers for local nonregular linear functionals.
Chernozhukov, Newey, Quintas-Martinez, and Syrgkanis (2021/2022) automate Riesz representer learning, adjacent to learned-localizer variants.
Semenova and Chernozhukov (2021) cover CATE projections and simultaneous inference rather than unprojected pointwise CATE.
Kallus, Mao, and Uehara (2024) provide a localized-DML target-dependent nuisance template, but not a matched pointwise CATE minimax theorem.
Benkeser, Cai, and van der Laan (2024) motivate adaptive nuisance selection; it remains secondary to the fixed-rate pointwise CATE benchmark.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Conjecture 2 / Assumption local-rich
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Assumption local-rich supplies the perturbability regime but does not itself exhibit the three Le Cam pairs.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The matching converse is accepted only as a field-tier conditional lower bound under the stated local-richness regime, not as a universal lower-bound packing for every black-box oracle model.

banked_reason: Originally ran (-0.5 ACCEPT, 0.5 ACCEPT@field); the field tier was a D-0.5 reviewer MISS. Topic is self-contradictory: structure-agnostic (no-smoothness) pointwise CATE tau_P(x0) is not a well-defined law functional. Corrected D-0.5 reviewer (well-definedness gate, 2026-06-04) returns REVISE/C-wellposed -> below field.
